Bookcases in Chetham's Library: Wikimedia Commons

Archives

For Speakers, Teachers and Learners

I want to give you stories, object lessons, and other things that might help you share the gospel of Christ with others. I plan to include topics, such as: Charity, Chastity, Christ, Commandments, Courage, Diligence, Faith, Forgiveness, Founding Fathers Wisdom, Hope, Humility, Kindness, Knowledge, Love, Marriage & Family, Miracles, Obedience, Patience, Persistence, Prayer, Purity, Scriptures, Speaking, Submission, Temperance, Teaching, Thanksgiving, Theism & Atheism, Virtue, Wisdom, etc.

Inspirational Stories Author
Miracle Meal for 600 Unexpected Guests Stephen M. Bird
Prayer Turned Our Marriage Around Stephen M. Bird
An Honored and Invited Guest Naida Stephens Tims retold by Stephen M. Bird
The Miracle Garden James Maybury as told to Stephen M. Bird
Marriage & Family Author
Toughing It Out: Happy Marriages Need Time to Grow Ramona Zabriskie
How Not to Talk to Your Kids: The Inverse Power of Praise Po Brunson
Prayer Can Bring Miracles to our Marriages Stephen M. Bird
Half a Pair of Scissors Richard and Linda Eyre
Marriage Makes Us Richer: Commandments, Marriage and Prosperity, Part 1 Stephen M. Bird
Marriage Makes Us Richer, Part 2 Stephen M. Bird
Religion & Society Author
Every Day Is Thanksgiving Stephen M. Bird
Humility Precedes the Miracles: Part 1 Stephen M. Bird
Our Christmas Angels Stephen M. Bird
Two Tales of 300 Warriors: Greeks and Israelites Stephen M. Bird
Jesus Christ: Easter, One Solitary Life Talk by Stephen M. Bird
Stolen Watch, Broken Heart Jarren Bird with comments by Stephen M. Bird
I Was a Bishop Before I Really Learned to Pray Richard D. Anthony
Three Great Reasons to Give Thanks John Hilton III and Anthony Sweat
What Is Mighty Prayer? Kersten Campbell
Mighty Prayer Brings the Spirit Stephen M. Bird
The Joy of Becoming Grateful Natalie Wilson
Financial Ark Author
Confession: I Was a Financial Disaster Andrea at SoOverDebt
Reading Author

PRAYERS THAT BRING MIRACLES

In the spring of 1983, I finished my Master's degree in counseling at BYU and was accepted into the Navy as an LDS Chaplain. My first assignment was with the 3rd Marine Infantry Battalion at Camp Pendleton, California. One Friday during lunch, a young black Marine knocked at my office door. I could tell from his troubled countenance that he was in trouble.

Read More »

ABOUT ME

I was born and raised in Ogden, Utah. I graduated from Bonneville High School in June of 1967 and attended Weber State College for a year before serving an LDS mission to Los Angeles, California from June of 1968 to June of 1970. Later, during the Viet Nam War, my draft number (#20) inspired me to enlist in the army as a Vietnamese linguist to avoid being drafted into the infantry.

Read More »
